items.summary: Redesign the StoriesStrip component to replace circular avatar bubbles (Instagram-style) with a horizontal card strip using rectangular or pill-shaped story cards that display the user's name below a square/rounded-rectangle thumbnail with a distinct colored border indicator for unseen stories.

Redesign the PostCard component to move away from the Instagram-style card layout. Use a content-first layout: place the post text/image prominently at the top, move the author info (avatar, display name, username, timestamp) into a compact inline row below the content, and replace the three-dot overflow menu with an inline icon row for actions. The like and comment counts should be displayed as pill badges rather than plain text counters.

Redesign the ProfilePage to replace the Instagram-style 3-column post grid and top stats row. Use a masonry or 2-column asymmetric grid for posts, and display follower/following stats as large typographic counters in a horizontal band with labels beneath them rather than as small inline numbers. The edit profile button should be a full-width outlined button styled with the app's gradient theme.

Redesign the BottomNav to use a floating pill-shaped navigation bar instead of a full-width flat tab bar. The nav should float above the bottom edge with a shadow and backdrop blur effect. The active tab indicator should be a filled background highlight within the pill rather than an underline or icon color change. The Create Post button should be an elevated square icon button within the pill.

Redesign the HomePage feed layout so it does not resemble Instagram's feed. Use a card-based feed with visible card background panels (using the app's glass/gradient card style) and left-accented colored borders per post type (text vs. image). Add a visual divider between the stories strip and the feed. Remove any full-bleed edge-to-edge image layout that mimics Instagram.

Redesign the LandingPage hero section to use an angled split layout or asymmetric two-panel design rather than a centered hero. The left/top panel should show the app logo and tagline; the right/bottom panel should show a stylized feature showcase or gradient graphic element. Replace any generic rounded CTA button with a distinctive styled button using the app's gradient and a unique shape (e.g., chamfered corners or arrow suffix).
